description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a rugged, natural appearance and a reddish-brown coloration. Its surface is uneven, featuring a mix of rounded and angular contours, with some shallow depressions and a few sharper ridges. The stone displays subtle mottling and color variations, suggesting mineral inclusions and natural weathering. One side appears slightly concave, possibly from erosion or impact, while the rest of the stone is more convex and bulbous. The texture is coarse and granular, with visible pitting and roughness typical of sedimentary or weathered igneous rock. This stone would be commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or as part of landscaping features along sidewalks or plazas.
description: A rugged, reddish-brown stone with an irregular, coarse surface and natural weathering, featuring both convex and concave areas.
description_view_0: The stone appears bulbous and rounded, with a prominent ridge and subtle mottling on its surface.
description_view_1: This view highlights a sloped, angular side with a visible depression and more pronounced surface roughness.
description_view_2: The stone's concave area is most visible here, showing a shallow basin-like depression and color variation.
description_view_3: A broad, convex surface dominates this view, with less visible pitting and a smoother overall contour.
attribute_location: urban park landscaping or garden path
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or partially embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle mottling and mineral speckling with irregular, natural color variations; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and pitted with visible signs of natural weathering, including minor surface erosion and color fading.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other landscaping stones; may contrast with concrete or metal urban features but integrates visually with vegetation and earth.
